label: Simple canker sore without systemic symptoms
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple canker sore or aphthous ulcer.
No fever, rash, ocular symptoms, genital ulcers, immunocompromised-host pathway, deep-space infection, or airway concern.
exclusion_criteria: Fever, rash, diarrhea, headache with systemic syndrome, ocular symptoms, genital ulcers, or recurrent severe ulcers.
Deep-space dental/neck infection, airway symptoms, dehydration, or immunocompromised host.
Suspicion for herpes, zoster, Stevens-Johnson syndrome, or malignancy.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, dentistry, or ENT if the sore is not improving, lasts more than 2 weeks, or recurs often.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports canker-sore and aphthous-ulcer framing, expected self-resolution, gentle oral care and symptom relief concepts, and escalation for ulcers lasting more than two weeks, worsening, frequent recurrence, or associated fever, diarrhea, headache, or rash. Autonomous Phase 1501-1550 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple canker sore without systemic features, ocular or genital involvement, dehydration, airway/deep-space concern, immunocompromise, or recurrent severe-ulcer pathway.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports canker-sore and aphthous-ulcer framing, expected self-resolution, gentle oral care and symptom relief concepts, and escalation for ulcers lasting more than two weeks, worsening, frequent recurrence, or associated fever, diarrhea, headache, or rash. Autonomous Phase 1501-1550 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed simple canker sore without systemic features, ocular or genital involvement, dehydration, airway/deep-space concern, immunocompromise, or recurrent severe-ulcer pathway.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of simple canker sore or aphthous ulcer
no systemic or dangerous oral features documented
no ocular, genital, airway, deep-space, dehydration, immunocompromised-host, or recurrent severe-ulcer pathway
